Faber2026 · joint time–frequency campaign · group-meeting update · 2026-07-17 (session 2)

Can we fit α < 4? — scattering-model physics, a campaign refit, and a decisive answer

Owner question → Cordes et al. 2025 theory → power-law-PBF model change (owner-approved) → injection controls → relaxed-α experiment: ΔlnZ = +5537 against our own model's self-consistency tie. Plus: first interior screen measurements, a corner-flip, a component-count audit, and a 3.16× speed fix.

Companions: model-change brief · validation figures · triptychs · phase-A deck

2 / 15

The question

“If we don't fit an EMG, can we fit for α < 4?”

Our joint model convolves a Gaussian burst with an exponential pulse-broadening function (the EMG) and ties the scaling index to the screen index: α = 2β/(β−2), prior α∈[4,6]. Three bursts rail at α=4. The owner asked whether dropping the EMG opens α<4, pointing to the Cordes, Ocker, Chatterjee et al. 2025 PTA-noise preprint (retrieved in full via the my-know-it-all service-account route; LaTeX source + figures now local).

Three load-bearing claims from the paper:

  • §12.6.4: “it is inconsistent to use an exponential PBF and any frequency scaling other than τ ∝ ν−4” — the exponential PBF is the β→4 limit. Our rail is the fit landing on the model's only self-consistent point.
  • Thin-screen theory has a floor: xτ = 2β/(β−2) ≥ 4 for every β. No homogeneous thin screen produces α<4 under any PBF.
  • Measured sub-4 indices (Löhmer’s 3.44±0.13) are truncated-screen physics or — more often — an artifact of fitting heavy-tailed scattering with an exponential PBF (§12.7, Fig 58).

defendclaims verified against the full text, not summaries — the MCP index only had shallow digests, so the answer required the document itself.

self-critiquethe preprint is a working draft: I found an internal inconsistency (§12.7 body says ld∝ν22/5; the Fig 58 caption and §12.6.4 both say ν6/5). We use the caption form, corroborated twice.

3 / 15

The model change (owner-approved)

The power-law PBF family — and our kernel reproducing it exactly

Cordes Fig 40: exponential core → t−β/2 tail → inner-scale cutoff.

Our kernel: same three regimes, peel-off ordered by the cutoff lag si.

The change is an extension, not a rewrite: FLITS already had the core+tail form; the new physics is the inner-scale cutoff (τi = ζ−2τ, applied to the tail branch only) and its chromaticity ζ(ν) ∝ ν−2/(β−2). Nesting is exact to machine zero in both limits: si→∞ = production PBF, si≤sc (or β→4) = our current EMG.

defendinjection recovery caught a real bug (cutoff anchored at the crossover instead of si) before any science use — the validate-first gate did its job. Recovery now unbiased; the new parameter si is identifiable.

self-critiquemy τi/τ = ζ−2 derivation has the right scaling but a ~30× normalization offset against Fig 40 — the agent caught it; we fit si directly and quote ζ only as an order-of-magnitude relabel.

4 / 15

Injection control

The wrong-PBF α artifact is bandwidth-lever-arm-dependent

Fitting our EMG to a heavy-tailed injection (true α=4.67): the joint CHIME+DSA fit recovers α to −0.06; a DSA-only fit (15% fractional bandwidth) collapses below 4 with wide errors — the Cordes Fig-58 artifact, reproduced in our own band configuration. Two paper-ready points: narrow-band single-instrument scattering indices are suspect as a class, and the joint methodology is the defense.

self-critiquethese are nlive=40–60 smokes (authoritative nlive=250 grid queued); and the injected tail was mild (si=10, single component) — which matters on slide 6.

5 / 15

Reading the rail correctly

Production “α = 4.00 ± 0.00” was definitional, not measured

Code audit found alpha_from_beta clamps to exactly 4.0 for β≥3.98 — inside the rail, α is pinned by construction. The corner shows it: α a clamped spike, β a uniform pile-up against its prior ceiling, τ1GHz a genuine measurement. Red/blue: the RFI-binning fix does not move the screen solution (3/3 across casey, chromatica, wilhelm — evidence improves by +582…+792 without moving β).

Consequence adopted campaign-wide: railed bursts report one-sided limits (β>3.98 ⇒ α<4.00 at 95%; phineas’s old solution the complementary corner), with τ1GHz as the measurement. The relaxed-α run became the only route to an unclamped α constraint — next slide.

6 / 15

The experiment that answers the question

Relaxed-α result: the data reject our own self-consistency tie — decisively

fitαβτ1GHz (ms)lnZΔlnZ vs clamped
casey, production (clamped)4.000 (definitional)3.990 (rail)0.0190−33574.6—
casey, free α ∈ [2,6]2.429 ± 0.0153.666 ± 0.007 (= Kolmogorov)0.059−28038.0+5537
wilhelm, production (clamped)4.000 (definitional)3.990 (rail)0.330−16496.2—
wilhelm, free α2.572 ± 0.0383.965 (still ceiling)0.251−15765.2+731

What “relaxed” means: the pulse shape is still an EMG at every frequency (Gaussian ⊗ exponential PBF); the only thing freed is the chromatic index α in τ∝ν−α, which production ties to β via the exponential-PBF self-consistency relation. That makes this a diagnostic wedge, not a physical model — an exponential PBF with α≠4 is internally inconsistent by construction (Cordes §12.6.4). Discipline: α≈2.5 is not a physical screen index — no thin screen reaches sub-4 (slide 2). Per the pre-registered interpretation matrix, a free α falling below 4 is the wrong-PBF mismatch signature on real data: α is absorbing the tail-shape error (and possibly other unmodeled chromatic terms). The comparison is a valid Bayes factor — the clamped model is the α=α(β) submanifold of the free family, same data and likelihood.

The scale is the surprise: injections predicted joint-fit bias of only −0.06 on a mild tail; the real shift is −1.6. Either casey’s true tail is far harsher than injected, or α is soaking up additional chromatic physics. Both readings say the same thing: the EMG family is no longer tenable for these bursts; the PL-PBF three-way comparison (clamped EMG / free-α EMG / physical heavy tail with α>4) is now the paper's model-selection story.

Update (landed after first publish): freeing α RESOLVES the tail structure, not just lnZ. Peak-window residual RMS: casey C 7.4→4.7 (max|r| 32→17), casey D 2.2→1.1, wilhelm C 2.3→1.2 — the wilhelm-C peak-undershoot/decay-overshoot pattern is fully resolved by the free-α model. The lone survivor is wilhelm’s DSA single-bin dipole (5.5→5.1, still ±20σ): untouched by α, therefore a separate non-scattering systematic, not part of the PBF story.

Update — the harsh-tail injections have now run, and they FAIL to reproduce the −1.6: even a tail running 1000 e-folds at casey-matched pulse width biases joint-fit α by only −0.01 (si-independent); the most favorable case in the family (narrow pulse, W/τ=0.3) reaches −0.21 — still 8× short. Directional controls (Fig 58 shallow-ward bias, Fig 54 τ overestimate) both pass: the mechanism is real, the magnitude is not. Single-component tail grossness is therefore ruled out as the sole driver of casey’s α=2.43 — the free α is absorbing something more (leading candidate: multi-component leakage, the wilhelm-C precedent; the pre-agreed harness extension to test it is now triggered). This sharpens the stakes of the three-way: if casey’s mismatch is not primarily tail shape, PL-PBF may not rescue α>4 there — wilhelm remains the stronger heavy-tail candidate.

self-critiqueremaining soft: casey’s free-α β landing exactly on 11/3 is suggestive, not yet an argument; the narrow-pulse case showing more bias than the wide one is opposite the Fig-54-motivated design expectation and needs a written rationale.

7 / 15

Mass-refit · first light

freya: the campaign’s first interior screen measurement

α = 4.357 ± 0.018   β = 3.697 ± 0.013   τ1GHz = 0.1158 ms   χ² C/D = 1.37/1.05

“Interior” = the β posterior sits away from the prior boundaries (the β=4 ceiling, the β=3 floor) — a genuine two-sided measurement rather than a rail pile-up. It is not a synonym for Kolmogorov (one specific value, β=11/3≈3.667); freya happens to be both: interior and 2.3σ from Kolmogorov, ~23σ below the ceiling. This is the existence proof that the family resolves interior β when the data support it: the three rails are burst-specific statements, not a methodology artifact. The per-channel residual strip at the CHIME peak is unmodeled scintillation (freya has 607 MHz scintle detections in the 2L table) — a cross-lane consistency point, not a defect.

The complete landscape: four interior measurements clustering near Kolmogorov, three ceiling-adjacent, three ceiling rails, and a floor-rail class — hamilton (β=3.003, with τ a fraction of a time bin) now joins whitney as its sibling, so the β=3 corner is a repeated mode, not a one-off. hamilton’s mid-run dlogz anomaly resolved into this floor solution. Numbers-only here pending triptych component-vetting; zach’s row is the D3 baseline your morphology call put under test (C2D4 running).

9 / 15

A cautionary result

phineas flipped corners under the RFI fix — β 3.01 → 3.96, τ 0.725 → 0.452 ms

The buggy-binning fit railed at the steep corner (β→3, α≈5.9); mask-aware binning moved it across the entire prior to ceiling-adjacent. So the RFI fix can flip a screen solution outright — the 3/3 "fix doesn't un-rail" result on casey/chromatica/wilhelm was correct but not universal. The 38% τ change propagates into phineas’s TOA scattering correction.

self-critiquethe pre-fix phineas samples were overwritten before anyone snapshotted them — the overlay above is reconstructed from logged percentiles, not a joint posterior. Remedy now standing: snapshot-before-overwrite for all bursts (johndoeII’s was captured minutes before its refit landed; it didn’t flip — β=3.771). whitney has meanwhile inherited the steep corner (β=3.025, ~1.3σ off the floor) and gets the same rail-gate scrutiny once its count tests land — and hamilton has since landed on the β=3 floor outright (slide 8), making the steep corner a class of two.

10 / 15

Owner catch · component counts

zach (FRB 20220207C): 1 + cluster of 3 — confirmed at native resolution

Owner ground truth (4 DSA components) verified on our own data: c2/c3/c4 at +2.06/+2.52/+3.01 ms after the initial pulse at native 32.8 µs. The lower panel now overlays the landed D3 baseline’s fitted t0’s (blue: +0.06/+2.55/+2.99): D3 spends its three components on the initial pulse + the +2.52 and +3.01 members and leaves the +2.06 member unfit (magenta) — even though it is still a distinct data peak at the fit’s 131 µs binning. So the miss is a model-count shortfall, not resolution loss: three components simply cannot cover a 1+3 structure. (This sharpens the earlier “c4 merges into c3’s shoulder” reading, which understated it.)

Remedy in flight: zach C2D4 queued behind its D3 baseline, with a pre-agreed lever — if the cluster members degenerate at 131 µs, drop the D-band binning one level rather than fight it with priors.

11 / 15

What the catch exposed

Count-ladder audit: D4 was never launched — for any burst

D-countruns ever launched
D117
D213
D36
D40

Only isha and whitney ever ran genuine count comparisons (both capped at D2). Everything else — including zach C2D3 and phineas C3D3 — ran at a single hand-assigned count. No burst could have been found to need D4.

Remedy (running): fixed-s2 neighbor tests (count+1 per band with count ≥2) for zach, phineas, whitney, johndoeII — eleven fits at s2=100, the setting most hostile to extra components, so any ΔlnZ>5 win for a higher count is conservative; marginal cases re-checked at s2=10; consistent with whitney’s pre-existing ladder. Multi-component TOA entries wait on these; the audit doc (COMPONENT_COUNT_LADDER_AUDIT.md) is written for the methods appendix — the honest answer to “how were counts chosen?”

self-critiquethis gap survived multiple reviews of the campaign design, including mine; it took the owner’s morphological knowledge of one burst to expose it.

12 / 15

Standing directive adopted

Component vetting is now part of figure publication

Owner directive: before any fit figure ships, visually vet the underlying fit for left-out components — band-summed data-vs-model + normalized residuals per band, count annotated; ladder verdicts don’t substitute. Applied retroactively to everything published today; standing for all 12 triptychs.

It immediately produced the campaign’s most interesting ambiguity: wilhelm’s CHIME band — the model undershoots the peak (+7.6σ narrow run) and overshoots the decay. The ladder rejected a second component; visually, an unfit narrow component riding a broad base is a live alternative. The two readings are discriminated by the PL-PBF test: sharp peak + fast early decay + long late tail is precisely the heavy-tail signature — and if the EMG’s forced-broad peak is what made one component look insufficient, count and shape were never independent questions.

13 / 15

Throughput

Speed work: 25% of the node was idle, and the FFT had a prime-length trap

Scheduler (immediate ~2× wall): jobs requested 3 GB/CPU; actual use ~0.8. Slurm’s ledger filled at 90/95 G while 10 of 40 CPUs idled and light bursts queued serially. Enforcement was off (ConstrainRAMSpace=no) — requests were pure bookkeeping — so pending jobs were dropped to 512/128 MB: 5→8 fits running within minutes, node packed 40/40. Submit scripts fixed at source.

Likelihood (3.16× measured, exact): profiling the full likelihood (not the kernel microbench) showed 78% of runtime in three FFTs whose length 2T hits a large prime whenever the auto-window makes T prime (freya: T=257 → ~13× slowdown). Fix: pad to next_fast_len(2T) — mathematically exact, max|ΔlogL| = 4×10−11 over 1000 posterior samples. Deployed mid-campaign with pre/post provenance; running fits untouched.

levergainrisk
memory-ledger fix~2× wall (tail)none (bookkeeping only)
next_fast_len FFT3.16× per fitnone (exact, 10−15)
relaxed-α + PL-PBF lanesinherit both—

self-critiquemy first speed hypothesis (JIT the erfcx loop) was wrong — a microbenchmark artifact (accidental power-of-2 sizes). Profiling the real call path found the actual bottleneck; the JIT would have bought 1.08×. Also: the owner had to push (“too long, can’t believe it”) before I went looking — the idle cores were findable an hour earlier.

14 / 15

Honesty section

What I got wrong today, and what remains soft

  • Wrong speed hypothesis — blamed the erfcx loop; real cause was prime-length FFTs. Corrected by profiling, not by luck.
  • ζ normalization off ~30× — my si=ζ−2 mapping had the right scaling, wrong constant vs Fig 40. Agent caught it; we now fit the cutoff lag directly.
  • Framed a “profiled s2 optimum” that doesn’t exist — production uses a flat gain prior; the agent corrected the neighbor-test protocol to the fixed-s2 grid (whitney precedent).
  • Published corners before component-vetting them — the owner had to demand the check; it then immediately surfaced the wilhelm-C ambiguity. The standard is now standing.
  • The count-ladder gap — hand-assigned counts survived my reviews too; caught only by owner morphology knowledge.
  • phineas pre-fix posterior lost — snapshot protocol arrived one burst late; the flip record is percentile-level only.

Still soft: injection controls at smoke-level nlive (authoritative nlive=250 grid pending); the −1.6 shift now has a negative result — harsh-tail injections ran and max out at −0.21 (slide 6), so the mechanism is still open with multi-component leakage as lead candidate (extension triggered); zach D3-vs-D4 and whitney’s count/rail interplay unresolved; hamilton/chromatica/zach triptychs not yet component-vetted. Resolved since first publish: relaxed-α residuals + corners vetted and published (slide 6); hamilton’s anomalous dlogz converged to the β=3 floor rail. New catch (now closed): the freshly built PL-PBF real-data fitter inherited production’s α-clamp at β≥3.98 — a silent-but-finite model bias (frozen α strip + 0.021 step at 3.98) that finiteness smokes couldn’t see and that would have bitten wilhelm exactly where its posterior straddles 3.98. Caught in cross-review, fixed as an unclamped 2β/(β−2) subclass override (canonical code untouched), and re-validated: β=3.99→α=4.010 not 4.0, continuity across 3.98, chromatic si sign confirmed (DSA tail relatively longer than CHIME) — all before any launch.

15 / 15

Decision queue & what lands next

Where this goes

  • Compute in flight: zach C2D4 (the owner-morphology test); 11 neighbor-test fits; PL-PBF recovery grid (nlive=250); harsh-tail scale-closure injections (running). All 12 production refits have landed.
  • The critical experiment: PL-PBF real-data fits on casey + wilhelm — the three-way model selection (clamped EMG / free-α EMG / physical heavy tail) that decides the paper’s scattering story. The fitter is built, smoke-passed, and review-hardened (exact nesting to the production kernel where the tie agrees, so the three-way ΔlnZ is a valid Bayes factor; the inherited α-clamp caught and fixed pre-launch, slide 14); launch is one staged command, held until the recovery grid certifies unbiased (β, si) recovery.
  • Then: TOA table (limit framing, count-resolved multi-component entries, inflated errors referencing the wilhelm ambiguity explicitly), triptychs + corners for all 12, flags table.
  • Owner decisions pending: ratify the 2L scintillation table (separate lane); ratify the count-audit remediation as the standing method; eventually: does the PL-PBF family become the campaign default (uniform methodology) if it wins on casey/wilhelm?

All artifacts: brief · validation · triptychs · journal (docs/rse/journal.jsonl) · provenance docs on h17 (FFTFIX_DEPLOY_PROVENANCE.md, COMPONENT_COUNT_LADDER_AUDIT.md, RFI_FIX_FLIP_RECORD.md).
